hypothesis: Hypothesis 2 (Warm-Start Pivot Caching via eBPF Maps):** Altering the eBPF bytecode to integrate a `BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERCPU_ARRAY` lookup prior to the partitioning phase to act as a "warm-start" snapshot. By storing histogram counts and optimal pivot statistics from previous sorting passes in this map, the modified bytecode uses `bpf_tail_call` instructions with a dynamic annealing threshold ($\tau$) to skip redundant pivot calculations, directly passing the cached state to the partitioner to accelerate deep recursion branches.
